# Introduction

LED driver systems are widely used in lighting, displays, automotive applications, and many embedded systems. In modern aircraft, LED lighting systems are used for cabin illumination, safety lighting, signage, and many other functions. These systems must be highly reliable, efficient, and suitable for the demanding conditions of aviation.

Mixed-signal LED drivers combine analog current-driving circuits with digital control logic. The digital part is responsible for controlling PWM (Pulse-Width Modulation), managing configuration registers, coordinating interfaces, and ensuring correct timing behaviour. Before a mixed-signal integrated circuit can be manufactured, its digital subsystem needs to be carefully designed, verified, and validated through FPGA prototyping and behavioural modelling.

This thesis focuses on the initial design of the digital part of a mixed-signal LED driver. The work includes requirements analysis, behavioural modelling, RTL design in SystemVerilog, hardware validation on a GateMate FPGA, and using IIC-OSIC-TOOLS suite, including cocotb,and documenting the results using Quarto.
